title: CTET 2026
meta_description: CTET 2026 — Central Teacher Eligibility Test for teaching in KVS, NVS, and central government schools. Paper 1 and Paper 2 pattern, syllabus, eligibility, and preparation tips.

hero.one_liner: Mandatory eligibility test for teaching positions in Kendriya Vidyalayas, Navodaya Vidyalayas, and other central government schools

what_is_it: CTET (Central Teacher Eligibility Test) is conducted by CBSE to certify candidates for teaching positions in central government schools — Kendriya Vidyalayas (KVS), Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas (NVS), Army Schools, Tibetan Schools, and schools under the Delhi administration. CTET qualification is mandatory — you cannot be appointed as a teacher in these schools without clearing CTET.

CTET has two papers: Paper 1 (for Classes 1-5 — Primary level) and Paper 2 (for Classes 6-8 — Upper Primary level). You can appear for both papers to be eligible for teaching at both levels. Each paper has 150 MCQs to be completed in 2.5 hours. You need 60% (90/150) to qualify (55% for reserved categories).

A major benefit: CTET qualification is now valid for LIFETIME (changed from 7 years in 2021). Once you clear CTET, you're permanently eligible for central government school teaching positions whenever they arise. CTET is conducted twice a year, and you can attempt it unlimited times to improve your score.

CTET is an eligibility test, NOT a recruitment exam. Clearing CTET makes you eligible to apply for KVS/NVS/other teaching vacancies — actual selection depends on the specific recruitment exam conducted by KVS, NVS, or DSSSB. However, a high CTET score can give you an edge in recruitment.

key_details.label: Paper 1 (Classes 1-5)
key_details.value: Eligibility: 12th pass with 50%+ AND D.El.Ed/BTC/B.Ed (or appearing in final year)

key_details.label: Paper 2 (Classes 6-8)
key_details.value: Eligibility: Graduation with 50%+ AND B.Ed (or appearing in final year). OR Graduation with 45% + NCTE-recognized teacher training

key_details.label: Attempts
key_details.value: Unlimited — no restriction on number of attempts

exam_pattern.description: 150 MCQs across 5 sections. All questions carry 1 mark. No negative marking.
150 MCQs. Same structure but with subject-specific section (Math/Science OR Social Studies) instead of EVS.

extra_sections.heading: CTET → KVS/NVS Teaching — Career Path
content: Step 1: Clear CTET Paper 1 (for PRT) and/or Paper 2 (for TGT eligibility).
Step 2: Apply for KVS, NVS, or DSSSB teaching vacancies when notifications are released. These organizations conduct their own recruitment exams where CTET is a mandatory eligibility criterion.
Step 3: Clear the organization-specific exam (KVS PRT exam, NVS TGT exam, etc.) and attend interview.

KVS/NVS teaching positions are among the best government jobs in India — salary at Level 6-7 (₹38,000-60,000/month starting), posting at schools across India, summer and winter vacations, pension, medical, and housing. Teacher to Vice Principal to Principal promotions are available.

KVS PRT salary comparison: Starting basic ₹35,400 + DA (~50%) + HRA + Transport = ₹38,000-48,000/month. This is higher than many banking and SSC posts. Combined with school timings (8 AM-2 PM typically) and 2+ months of annual vacation, teaching is an excellent career choice.

important_dates.label: CTET 2026
important_dates.value: Conducted twice a year (check ctet.nic.in for exact dates)

important_dates.label: KVS/NVS recruitment
important_dates.value: Separate notification — check kvs.ac.in and navodaya.gov.in

preparation_tips: Child Development & Pedagogy (CDP) is common to both papers and is CTET-specific — you won't find this in any other competitive exam. Study Piaget, Vygotsky, Kohlberg, NCF 2005, RTE 2009, inclusive education, and learning theories. This section is scoring if you understand the concepts.

For Paper 1 Math and EVS, questions are at Class 1-5 level — very basic arithmetic, shapes, measurement, and environmental science. The tricky part is the pedagogy sub-questions in each section — how to TEACH the concepts, not just know them.

For Paper 2 Math/Science or Social Studies, questions are at Class 6-8 NCERT level. Read NCERT textbooks for Classes 6-8 thoroughly. Focus on understanding pedagogy — how students learn, common misconceptions, and activity-based learning approaches.
No negative marking means you should attempt ALL 150 questions. Even intelligent guessing helps. Don't leave any question unanswered — there's no penalty for wrong answers.

faqs.question: Is CTET enough to become a teacher?
faqs.answer: CTET makes you ELIGIBLE to apply for teaching positions in central government schools. Actual appointment requires clearing the specific recruitment exam (KVS exam, NVS exam, DSSSB exam, etc.). Think of CTET as a license to practice — the job itself requires another selection process.

faqs.question: I have B.Ed but no CTET. Can I teach?
faqs.answer: Not in central government schools (KVS, NVS). CTET is mandatory. However, many state government and private schools accept B.Ed without CTET. States have their own TET exams (UPTET, MPTET, REET, etc.) which are required for state government school appointments.

faqs.question: Can I appear for both Paper 1 and Paper 2?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can appear for both papers in the same CTET cycle. This makes you eligible for both Primary (Classes 1-5) and Upper Primary (Classes 6-8) teaching positions — giving you more job options.

faqs.question: CTET vs State TET — which should I take?
faqs.answer: Take BOTH if possible. CTET qualifies you for central schools (KVS, NVS — pan-India posting). State TET qualifies you for state government schools (posting within your state). Having both gives maximum flexibility. The syllabus overlaps 80%+.

faqs.question: What is the qualifying score?
faqs.answer: General: 90/150 (60%). OBC/SC/ST/PwBD: 82/150 (55%). There's no ranking — it's pass/fail. Everyone who scores above the cutoff is 'CTET qualified'. However, a higher score may give weightage in some recruitment processes like KVS/NVS.
